preamble: The transformation of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] into the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] is a documented form of gutturalization, typically occurring as a multi-stage process where sibilants shift to rhotics which then retract to uvular positions.

examples.note: Widespread areal shift where apical rhotics (often derived from historical sibilants) shifted to uvular fricative realizations.
Historical development of voiced sibilants into rhotics, which are realized as uvular fricatives in standard and many dialectal varieties.

sources: Phonological research on the European rhotic transition.
